headline: CMS SignedData signing arrives as pki.cms.sign, and RFC 3161 timestamp token creation as pki.tsp.sign -- the producing sides of the CMS and timestamp verifiers, emitting exactly what pki.cms.verify and OpenSSL cms -verify accept.
summary: pki.cms.sign(content, signers, opts) produces a CMS SignedData (RFC 5652 section 5) -- the structure S/MIME signed mail, code signing, and timestamp tokens rest on. It builds the signed attributes (content-type, message-digest, signing-time, plus any caller-supplied attribute) as canonical DER, signs the exact RFC 5652 section 5.4 preimage, and emits a DER Buffer or PEM. Attached and detached content, single and multiple signers, RSA (PKCS#1 v1.5 and RSASSA-PSS), ECDSA (P-256/384/521), Ed25519, and Ed448 are covered; the signer identifier is issuerAndSerialNumber or subjectKeyIdentifier. Built atop it, pki.tsp.sign(messageImprint, tsa, opts) creates an RFC 3161 TimeStampToken: a SignedData whose content is a TSTInfo carrying the timestamped message imprint, the TSA policy, a serial number, and genTime (with optional accuracy, nonce, and ordering), plus the RFC 3161 section 2.4.2 / RFC 5816 signing-certificate attribute binding the token to the TSA certificate. Both producing paths emit exactly the shapes their verifiers check -- canonical DER signed attributes, the same algorithm-parameter forms (NULL for RSA, absent for ECDSA/EdDSA, the RSASSA-PSS parameters), and ECDSA signatures re-encoded to canonical DER through the shared DER-ECDSA gate -- so a message this toolkit signs round-trips through pki.cms.verify and is accepted by OpenSSL cms -verify.

Added: pki.cms.sign(content, signers, opts) produces a CMS SignedData (RFC 5652 section 5): attached or detached content, one or many signers, RSA (PKCS#1 v1.5 and, with opts.pss, RSASSA-PSS), ECDSA (P-256/384/521), Ed25519, and Ed448. Each signer is { cert, key } (a PEM/DER certificate and a WebCrypto CryptoKey or PKCS#8 key); the signed attributes (content-type, message-digest, signing-time, plus opts.additionalSignedAttributes) are canonical DER and the signature covers the exact section 5.4 preimage. opts selects detached content, the encapsulated content type, the signer identifier (issuerAndSerialNumber or subjectKeyIdentifier), certificate embedding, and DER or PEM output.

pki.tsp.sign(messageImprint, tsa, opts) creates an RFC 3161 TimeStampToken over a message imprint ({ hashAlgorithm, hashedMessage }): a CMS SignedData whose content is a TSTInfo carrying the imprint, the TSA policy, a serial number, and genTime, with optional accuracy, nonce, and ordering, and the RFC 3161 section 2.4.2 / RFC 5816 signing-certificate (ESSCertIDv2) attribute binding the token to the TSA certificate. It composes pki.cms.sign, so any supported TSA key algorithm works.
